name: Rest, Sleep & Balance
description: Permission to slow down — quotes on the quiet art of rest, sleep, and a life in balance.
author: Quips Editorial

quotes.content: Take rest; a field that has rested gives a bountiful crop.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the Roman poet; a widely used rendering on the necessity of rest.

quotes.content: Sleep that knits up the ravell'd sleave of care, the death of each day's life, sore labour's bath, balm of hurt minds, great nature's second course, chief nourisher in life's feast.
quotes.notes: Macbeth's anguished praise of the sleep he has murdered.

quotes.content: Early to bed and early to rise makes a man healthy, wealthy, and wise.
quotes.notes: One of the best-known maxims from Franklin's annual almanac.

quotes.content: Rest is not idleness, and to lie sometimes on the grass under trees on a summer's day, listening to the murmur of the water, or watching the clouds float across the sky, is by no means a waste of time.
quotes.notes: From the English polymath's book of practical wisdom.

quotes.content: There is a time for many words, and there is also a time for sleep.
quotes.notes: Odysseus's words in Homer's epic; wording varies by translation.

quotes.content: It is a common experience that a problem difficult at night is resolved in the morning after the committee of sleep has worked on it.
quotes.notes: From Steinbeck's novel; the origin of the phrase 'the committee of sleep.'

quotes.content: If we do not allow for a rhythm of rest in our overly busy lives, illness becomes our Sabbath.
quotes.source: Sabbath: Finding Rest, Renewal, and Delight in Our Busy Lives (1999)
quotes.notes: From the minister and therapist's meditation on the lost practice of rest.

quotes.content: Caring for myself is not self-indulgence, it is self-preservation, and that is an act of political warfare.
quotes.notes: From the poet and activist's essays written during her illness.

quotes.content: We think, mistakenly, that success is the result of the amount of time we put in at work, instead of the quality of time we put in.
quotes.notes: From the media founder's book redefining success beyond money and power.

quotes.content: He that can take rest is greater than he that can take cities.
quotes.notes: Another of Franklin's maxims on the value of rest over conquest.

quotes.content: Sleep is the golden chain that ties health and our bodies together.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the English dramatist, often linked to his pamphlet The Gull's Hornbook.

quotes.content: Each night, when I go to sleep, I die. And the next morning, when I wake up, I am reborn.
quotes.notes: Attributed to Gandhi on renewal and the daily cycle of rest.

quotes.content: That we are not much sicker and much madder than we are is due exclusively to that most blessed and blessing of all natural graces, sleep.
quotes.notes: From the author's collection of essays.

quotes.content: A well-spent day brings happy sleep.
quotes.notes: Attributed to da Vinci; a saying often drawn from his notebooks.

quotes.content: Sleep is the best meditation.
quotes.notes: Widely attributed to the 14th Dalai Lama.

quotes.content: No day is so bad it can't be fixed with a nap.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the American comedian.

quotes.content: Rest when you're weary. Refresh and renew yourself, your body, your mind, your spirit. Then get back to work.
quotes.notes: From the writer behind the long-running Daily Motivator column.

quotes.content: Sometimes the most important thing in a whole day is the rest we take between two deep breaths.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the Dutch writer whose wartime diaries were published as An Interrupted Life.

quotes.content: Rest is the sweet sauce of labour.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the Greek biographer and essayist.

quotes.content: A ruffled mind makes a restless pillow.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the author of Jane Eyre.

quotes.content: I love sleep. My life has the tendency to fall apart when I'm awake, you know?
quotes.notes: Widely attributed to Hemingway; a primary source has not been confirmed.

quotes.content: Rest and self-care are so important. When you take time to replenish your spirit, it allows you to serve others from the overflow. You cannot serve from an empty vessel.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the author; the closing line is frequently quoted on its own.

quotes.content: For fast-acting relief, try slowing down.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the comedian and actress.

quotes.content: Learn to say 'no' without explaining yourself.
quotes.notes: Attributed to the author of The Single Woman, on setting healthy boundaries.

quotes.content: Finish each day and be done with it. You have done what you could. Some blunders and absurdities no doubt crept in; forget them as soon as you can. Tomorrow is a new day.
quotes.notes: Attributed to Emerson; a favorite passage on letting the day go.

quotes.content: Time you enjoy wasting is not wasted time.
quotes.notes: From Troly-Curtin's novel; often misattributed to Bertrand Russell or John Lennon.

quotes.content: One of the symptoms of an approaching nervous breakdown is the belief that one's work is terribly important.
quotes.notes: From the philosopher's book on the causes of, and cures for, unhappiness.

quotes.content: How beautiful it is to do nothing, and then to rest afterward.
quotes.notes: A traditional Spanish saying ('Qué bonito es no hacer nada, y luego descansar').
